rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD MARSHALL DIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petitioner Aaron R. Granquists First Amended Petition Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850 For Claims Of Adverse Possession And Equitable Apportionment
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Respondent's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. (See Evid. Code, sections 452, 453.) Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Respondent's Demurrer to the Petition Pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 for Claim of Adverse Possession and Equitable Apportionment is in part SUSTAINED without leave to amend, pursuant to CCP section 430.10(e), and in part OVERRULED. The demurrer as to the First Cause of Action for adverse possession and relief pursuant to Probate Code section 850(a)(2(D) is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. In an action to quiet title based on adverse possession the burden is upon the claimant to prove every necessary element: (1) Possession must be by actual occupation under such circumstances as to constitute reasonable notice to the owner. (2) It must be hostile and exclusive to the owner's title. (3) The holder must claim the property as his own under either color of title or claim of right. (4) Possession must be continuous and uninterrupted for five years. (5) The holder must pay all the taxes levied and assessed upon the property during the period. (Dimmick v. Dimmick (1962) 58 Cal. 2d 417, 421.) The material facts properly pleaded on the face of the petition still do not adequately address the issue of Elinor's status as conservator. The Amended Petition acknowledges that Elinor was appointed as her brother's conservator in 1983 and remained in that role until the time of his death. (Amended Pet., par. 19.) The probate court records submitted by Respondent do not show that Elinor submitted a final accounting in the conservatorship, or that she was relieved as conservator. Therefore, as a matter of law, Elinor did not possess the subject property in a "hostile and exclusive" manner to other owners or claimants because she had continuing fiduciary duties as the court-appointed conservator of her brother's estate. As stated in Conservatorship of O'Connor (1996) 48 Cal. App. 4th 1076, 1089, "[A]lthough the trial court correctly observed that death of a conservatee terminates the conservatorship, the conservator has a continuing "duty of custody and conservation of the estate" after the conservatee's death "pending the delivery thereof to the personal representative of the ... conservatee's estate or other disposition according to law." (Prob. Code, section 2467, subd. (a).)" Having taken possession of the subject property as a court-appointed conservator, and having never been relieved of that role, Elinor did not possess the property in a manner hostile to Richard's intestate heirs, or arguable tenants in common. (See also Estate of Seifert (2005)128 Cal.App.4th 64, 68 ("[T]he rule is that the statute of limitations does not run where the parties occupy a fiduciary relationship toward each other, so long as such relationship is not repudiated. Thus, a guardian may not acquire title by adverse possession against his ward, nor an agent against his principal, nor an executor against the heirs."] [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD MARSHALL DIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petitioner Aaron R. Granquists First Amended Petition Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850 For Claims Of Adverse Possession And Equitable Apportionment
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] The demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for "equitable apportionment" under Probate Code section 850(a)(2)(D) in OVERRULED. The procedure available under Probate Code section 11700 is not the exclusive method for determining rights to estate property (see "may" in the statute), and all title disputes and adverse claims can be resolved under section 850(a)(2). Per the facts pleaded on the face of the Petition, Elinor's estate is an "interested party" to the administration of Richard's estate, and thus may file a statement of interest under section 11702, or a claim under section 850 et seq. However, although section 11700 is not the exclusive means to determine right to estate property, the order entered in a section 11700 proceeding is conclusive as to the rights of all interested persons and on any subsequent distribution proceedings (Prob. C. section 11705(b).) Respondent shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on April 7, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]
